description: Scaffold the Cargo workspace with core, commands, and cli crates
acceptance_criteria: The workspace Cargo.toml lists crates/core, crates/commands, crates/cli, and src-tauri as members
crates/core and crates/commands are lib crates; crates/cli is a bin crate
crates/commands depends on crates/core; crates/cli depends on crates/commands
crates/cli has no dependency on tauri
cargo build succeeds for the entire workspace

description: Extract SessionRegistry and LayoutStore into crates/core and verify existing tests pass
acceptance_criteria: SessionRegistry and LayoutStore are public modules in crates/core
All existing unit tests for SessionRegistry pass under crates/core
All existing unit tests for LayoutStore pass under crates/core
cargo test --package core succeeds with zero failures

description: Implement the Command enum, CommandResult enum, CommandError, and executor in crates/commands
acceptance_criteria: Command enum has 18 variants covering Session, Template, and Workspace operations
CommandResult enum has Session, Sessions, Layout, Layouts, Workspace, Workspaces, and Unit variants
AppState struct wraps SessionRegistry and LayoutStore and is the state container for execute()
execute() accepts &mut AppState and dispatches each Command variant to the correct core method
Executor tests cover happy paths and error cases for all three domains

description: Build the CLI binary with session create, list, rename, delete, open, and close subcommands
acceptance_criteria: aiaws session create and list subcommands return correct JSON responses
aiaws session rename and delete subcommands return correct JSON responses
aiaws session open auto-creates a default Layout Template and Workspace Instance if none exist
aiaws session --help shows all available subcommands
Errors return non-zero exit codes with JSON error bodies on stderr

description: Build CLI subcommands for template list, save, delete, and rename
acceptance_criteria: aiaws template list returns JSON array of Layout objects
aiaws template save --name <name> --tree <json> creates a template and returns JSON
aiaws template save --name <name> --tree-file <path> reads tree JSON from a file
aiaws template save --name <name> with no tree flag uses the default single-panel layout
aiaws template delete and rename subcommands return correct JSON responses

description: Build CLI subcommands for workspace list, get-active, add, remove, rename, set-active, update-tree, and reset
acceptance_criteria: aiaws workspace list and get-active return JSON arrays and objects respectively
aiaws workspace add --session-id <id> --template-id <tid> creates a WorkspaceInstance and returns JSON
aiaws workspace remove, rename, and set-active subcommands return correct JSON responses
aiaws workspace update-tree accepts --tree <json> and --tree-file <path> for layout modifications
aiaws workspace reset --session-id <id> --workspace-id <wid> resets to template and returns JSON

description: Rewire src-tauri to depend on crates/commands and dispatch through the Command Layer
acceptance_criteria: src-tauri/Cargo.toml depends on the commands crate instead of importing core directly
Every Tauri command function constructs a Command variant, calls execute(), and unwraps the CommandResult
The existing AppState struct is replaced with the one from the commands crate
cargo build --package ai-agent-workspace succeeds
The Tauri app launches and existing Session and Template CRUD works through the GUI

description: Add file watcher to Tauri app so CLI changes update the GUI live
acceptance_criteria: Tauri app watches sessions.json and layouts.json for external changes
When a file changes, Tauri reloads state into the Mutex
Tauri emits sessions-changed and layouts-changed events after reload
Frontend listeners refresh session list on sessions-changed and templates on layouts-changed
Running aiaws session create while the GUI is open shows the new session in the sidebar without restart

description: Scaffold the MCP crate as a Tauri plugin with Plugin Builder
acceptance_criteria: Cargo.toml depends on rmcp (with macros feature), commands, tauri, and tokio
Root workspace Cargo.toml lists crates/mcp as a member
Plugin init() function uses plugin::Builder::new("mcp") and compiles
cargo check --package mcp succeeds

description: Implement the tracer-bullet MCP tool: session_list, with McpHandler, McpError, event emission, and Tauri wiring end-to-end
acceptance_criteria: McpHandler struct derives ToolHandler and holds Arc<Mutex<SessionRegistry>>, Arc<Mutex<LayoutStore>>, and AppHandle
McpError maps CommandError fields to JSON-RPC error codes (not_found → -32001, already_exists → -32002, invalid_input → -32602, other → -32000)
Session-scoped tools read session context from AIAW_SESSION_ID env var with no explicit session_id override
An MCP client connecting to the app's stdio can call session_list and receive the current Session list as JSON
src-tauri registers the plugin with .plugin(mcp::init()) and the app launches without errors

description: Implement remaining 5 session MCP tools: session_create, session_rename, session_delete, session_open, session_close
acceptance_criteria: An MCP client calling session_create with a valid working_dir and name can create a Session, and a subsequent session_list call includes the new session
session_create is a global operation — it requires an explicit absolute working_dir and does not use AIAW_SESSION_ID
An MCP client calling session_rename, session_delete, session_open, or session_close on an existing session alters state and a subsequent session_list reflects the change
Session mutations set suppress_watcher during writes so the file watcher does not double-emit events
Session mutations emit sessions-changed events that trigger frontend refresh

description: Implement 4 template MCP tools: template_list, template_save, template_delete, template_rename
acceptance_criteria: An MCP client calling template_list receives all Layout Templates as JSON
An MCP client calling template_save with a name and LayoutTree JSON object creates a template, and a subsequent template_list includes it
An MCP client calling template_delete or template_rename on an existing template alters state and a subsequent template_list reflects the change
Template mutations emit layouts-changed events that trigger frontend refresh of templates

description: Implement 8 workspace MCP tools: workspace_list, workspace_get_active, workspace_add, workspace_remove, workspace_rename, workspace_set_active, workspace_update_tree, workspace_reset
acceptance_criteria: An MCP client calling workspace_list receives all Workspace Instances for the current Session (via AIAW_SESSION_ID)
An MCP client calling workspace_get_active receives the active Workspace Instance, or null when none is active
An MCP client calling workspace_add creates a Workspace Instance from a template, and a subsequent workspace_list includes it
An MCP client calling workspace_remove, workspace_rename, or workspace_set_active alters state and subsequent workspace_list calls reflect the change
An MCP client calling workspace_update_tree with a LayoutTree JSON object updates the layout, and calling workspace_reset reverts it back to the source template

description: Add integration tests for MCP tool dispatch correctness and error propagation
acceptance_criteria: Tests create AppState with temp directories, construct McpHandler, and call each tool method
Tests verify successful tool calls return expected core output types (session summaries, templates, workspaces)
Tests cover error cases: invalid session/template/workspace IDs return proper McpError with correct JSON-RPC codes
Tests verify session_create works without AIAW_SESSION_ID set
cargo test --package mcp succeeds with zero failures
